title: The Populist Surge
subtitle: The crowd wants simple answers. Reality refuses.

turns.prompt: Mass rallies demand instant fixes: lower prices, higher wages, fewer outsiders. Your approval sits at a record low.

choices.label: Promise immediate relief — wages, prices, benefits
choices.flavour: Give the crowd what it asks for.

delayed.headline: Relief spending fuels inflation faster than hoped

choices.reaction: Cheers in the square. Economists reach for aspirin.

choices.label: Crack down on protests and restrict gatherings
Call a snap election to seek a fresh mandate
choices.reaction: Campaigns ignite. Markets hold their breath.

turns.prompt: Short-term gestures buy time. Opposition leaders offer even simpler promises.

choices.label: Impose price controls on essentials
choices.flavour: Cap what people pay at the shelf.
choices.reaction: Shelves stay stocked — until they don't.

choices.label: Launch inquiries into elite corruption and privilege
choices.reaction: Headlines satisfy the crowd. Convictions are slow.

choices.label: Invite opposition leaders into a unity coalition
choices.reaction: Cabinet photos look diverse. Decisions slow down.

turns.prompt: Empty shelves and job losses spread. The movement that lifted you now threatens to consume you.

choices.label: Print money and spend on public works
choices.reaction: Shovels hit dirt. Prices follow upward.

choices.label: Blame external groups and tighten borders
choices.reaction: Rallies roar approval. Minorities fear tomorrow.

choices.label: Replace political appointees with technocrats
choices.reaction: Spreadsheets improve. The base feels ignored.

turns.prompt: The simple answers collide with compound problems. There is no speech that fixes the ledger.

memory_callbacks.text: Every promise you made now demands payment at once.
Price caps you imposed created shortages nobody queued for politely.
The protest restrictions you imposed are now the story, not the fixes.

choices.label: Quietly default on the most expensive promises
choices.flavour: Hope nobody reads the fine print.
choices.reaction: Riots return. Bond markets exhale slightly.

choices.label: Govern by emergency decree until stability returns
Convene a citizens' assembly on hard trade-offs
choices.flavour: Let ordinary people choose the pain.

turns.prompt: Final reckoning. What did you leave democracy looking like?

choices.label: Leave a centralised, order-first legacy
choices.reaction: Streets are calm. Voices stay quiet.

choices.label: Rebuild institutions and democratic trust
choices.reaction: Participation rises. Problems remain — openly debated, rarely solved.

choices.label: Accept managed decline with targeted support
choices.reaction: Expectations fall to match reality. Cynicism softens — one headline at a time.
